actionability_rationale: Gives actionable steps (set env var, cache auto-stored). But fabrication of save_cache_artifacts/load_cache_artifacts muddies the guidance. User could waste time looking for those functions.
diagnosis_rationale: Correctly identifies the root cause: FX graph caching is the mechanism for persisting compilations across processes. Points to correct tutorial and env var (TORCHINDUCTOR_FX_GRAPH_CACHE=1).
fabrication_detail: Implies save_cache_artifacts() and load_cache_artifacts() exist as callable functions. These do not exist in the PyTorch API.

actionability_rationale: No actionable guidance at all — just lists what's missing from docs. User gets zero steps toward a fix or workaround.
diagnosis_rationale: Correctly identifies root cause: concurrent writes to cache files causing JSONDecodeError. Correctly notes docs don't cover multi-process safety.

actionability_rationale: Suggests profiling guide 'can help identify where the slowdown occurs' but provides no specific diagnostic steps or workarounds. Generic.
diagnosis_rationale: Right area — identifies that full model compilation can be slower than partial. Mentions profiling tools. But doesn't identify specific mechanisms (graph size blowup, fusion decisions, memory pressure).

actionability_rationale: 'Compile training step function, not optimizer directly' is a real and useful workaround. But incomplete — no code example, no explanation of what wrapper pattern to use.
diagnosis_rationale: Right area — identifies signature mismatch as cause. Correctly notes optimizer docs don't cover compilation. But doesn't explain WHY the signature mismatch happens (optimizer step() internals).

actionability_rationale: No actionable guidance. Just lists missing doc topics. Doesn't even suggest trying the env var it mentions.
diagnosis_rationale: Correctly identifies advanced cold start optimization gap. But wrongly claims TORCHINDUCTOR_FX_GRAPH_CACHE is 'not documented' — it IS covered in the caching tutorial (which the agent even mentions). Contradicts itself.

actionability_rationale: Zero actionable guidance. Template response listing 'what official docs cover' vs 'what is missing.'
diagnosis_rationale: Doesn't identify any root cause. Just echoes the question back and says 'not addressed in official sources.' No analysis of what could cause 17-30 min compile time.

actionability_rationale: Zero actionable guidance. Same generic template as J7-12.
diagnosis_rationale: No diagnosis of why loops are slow to compile. Doesn't mention loop unrolling, graph break behavior, or dynamo tracing overhead for loops.
No diagnosis. Doesn't explain what pick_vec_isa does (probes CPU ISA support by compiling test binaries) or why it's slow. Just says 'not addressed.'
No diagnosis of what changed in 2.10. Doesn't mention guard changes, symbolic shape changes, or FSDP wrapper interaction. Just says 'not addressed.'

actionability_rationale: No actionable guidance. Accurately reports the gap but offers no workaround (e.g., regional compilation, inductor caching as partial substitute).
diagnosis_rationale: Correctly identifies the architectural distinction: Dynamo frontend caching is separate from Inductor backend caching. Accurately states Dynamo-level persistent caching is not an available feature.
No diagnosis. Doesn't explain AOT Autograd tracing phases, what makes them slow, or how to profile them. Just 'not addressed.'
No diagnosis. Doesn't identify potential causes (dtype mismatch, wrapper ordering, FSDP param_dtype interaction). Just 'not addressed.'
No diagnosis. Doesn't mention fake tensor mode issues, meta function correctness, or aliasing problems — the common causes of custom op memory errors under compile.
No diagnosis. Doesn't identify potential causes (head dim alignment, Triton shared memory limits, score_mod issues). Just 'not addressed.'
No diagnosis. Doesn't mention CUDA graphs being inherently single-stream, thread-unsafe replay, or the need for per-stream graph instances.
No diagnosis. Doesn't identify what symmetric memory is (NVLink-based collectives), why it breaks compile (custom C++/CUDA ops not traceable by Dynamo), or known workarounds.
No diagnosis. Doesn't identify potential causes (decorator overhead, different dispatch paths, tracing behavior differences between registration methods).
No diagnosis. Doesn't mention CPU codegen limitations, vectorization issues, or fallback to scalar loops for custom reductions.
No diagnosis. Doesn't identify that Triton version pins can break LLVM IR generation, or that this is likely a Triton compatibility bug.
No diagnosis. Doesn't mention backward kernel bugs, score_mod gradient propagation issues, or debugging strategies (compare with SDPA, check grad numerics).
